approaches.rationale: Neovim is a large CMake C project with a code-generation pipeline (src/nvim/generators), Lua/Luajit subsystems, treesitter, libuv, and many bundled deps fetched via cmake.deps — far beyond what gazelle_cc can model. The only credible port is to wrap its own CMake build as a single Bazel cmake() action.
approaches.module_bazel:
module(
    name = "neovim",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_foreign_cc", version = "0.15.1")
bazel_dep(name = "rules_cc", version = "0.2.19")
bazel_dep(name = "platforms", version = "1.1.0")
bazel_dep(name = "bazel_skylib", version = "1.9.0")

cc_configure = use_extension(
    "@rules_cc//cc:extensions.bzl",
    "cc_configure_extension",
)
use_repo(cc_configure, "local_config_cc")

foreign_cc = use_extension(
    "@rules_foreign_cc//foreign_cc:extensions.bzl",
    "tools",
)
use_repo(foreign_cc, "cmake_src", "ninja_build")
approaches.root_build:
load("@rules_foreign_cc//foreign_cc:defs.bzl", "cmake")

filegroup(
    name = "all_srcs",
    srcs = glob(
        ["**"],
        exclude = [
            "bazel-*/**",
            "build/**",
            ".git/**",
        ],
    ),
)

# Wraps Neovim's own CMake build. cmake.deps is a separate subproject that
# fetches/builds bundled third-party deps; this target builds the main tree
# and assumes deps are discoverable (USE_BUNDLED handled by CMake) or present
# on the toolchain sysroot. Coarse single wrapping target.
cmake(
    name = "nvim",
    lib_source = ":all_srcs",
    cache_entries = {
        "CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE": "RelWithDebInfo",
    },
    generate_args = ["-GNinja"],
    out_binaries = ["nvim"],
    binaries = ["nvim"],
    targets = ["nvim"],
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
)
anticipated_risks: code-gen (src/nvim/generators requires host Lua at configure time)
bundled deps via cmake.deps subproject (libuv, luajit, lpeg, treesitter, unibilium, utf8proc) not provided to the wrapped build
configure-time defines and Find modules expecting system libraries

approaches.rationale: A speculative source-native attempt: point gazelle_cc at the src/nvim C tree to generate cc_library targets from include graphs. Almost certainly under-models the generated headers and bundled-dep includes, but emitted to compare against the foreign-cc wrap.
approaches.module_bazel:
module(
    name = "neovim",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_cc", version = "0.2.19")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle_cc", version = "0.6.0")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")
bazel_dep(name = "platforms", version = "1.1.0")
bazel_dep(name = "bazel_skylib", version = "1.9.0")
bazel_dep(name = "rules_go", version = "0.61.1")

cc_configure = use_extension(
    "@rules_cc//cc:extensions.bzl",
    "cc_configure_extension",
)
use_repo(cc_configure, "local_config_cc")
approaches.root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")

# gazelle:cc_generate true
# gazelle:exclude runtime
# gazelle:exclude cmake.deps
# gazelle:exclude deps
# gazelle:exclude src/nvim/generators
# gazelle:exclude test

gazelle(
    name = "gazelle",
    gazelle = "@gazelle_cc//:gazelle_cc",
)
anticipated_risks: generated headers (auto/, config.h.in, versiondef.h.in) not present pre-build
code-gen pipeline produces dispatch/event/api sources that gazelle_cc cannot see
bundled third-party includes (libuv, luajit, treesitter, lpeg) unresolved
heavy macro/include indirection breaks include-based target inference
